title: Corneal abrasion follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

referral.text: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.
referral.rationale: Supports assessment and referral context for corneal abrasion follow-up; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

follow_up.text: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.
follow_up.rationale: Supports causes, symptoms and presentation for corneal abrasion follow-up; no decision is generated.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Eye, cause, date, pain, photophobia, foreign body, contact lenses, vision, staining, treatment, response and examination are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for corneal abrasion follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
